内部-未公开 / NON-PUBLIC — EMBARGO UNTIL 2026-07-15 09:00 CST — Deal Codename: Project Green Koi.
Board Briefing Memo: Cyber Incident at Yulan Metals Co., Ltd. (统一社会信用代码: 9 1 Y U L A N 0 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 0 X), A‑share issuer applicant to Lingnan Stock Exchange (LNX) under oversight of the Huaxia Securities Regulatory Authority (HSRA).
On 2026-06-28 23:14 CST, unauthorized access was detected in the payroll subsystem; 23 employee records accessed, including names, mobile numbers, and partially masked PRC ID numbers (e.g., 44************9X7); no minors under 14 were implicated per PIPL sensitive PI rules.
Two files were staged for cross-border transfer to a non-CN endpoint; transfer blocked and logged pending Donghai Municipal CAC security assessment; data localization and minimization steps implemented under CSL/DSL.
Preliminary loss estimate RMB 12,600,000, anticipated remediation spend RMB 3,200,000, and potential revenue variance of ~2.4% for Q3 due to delayed shipments from the Foshan plant.
This incident intersects ongoing M&A discussions — Project Green Koi term sheet (non-binding) targets execution of a definitive agreement by 2026-08-05 with a purchase price adjustment if cyber remediation exceeds RMB 5,000,000 or >1.0% of net assets.
Counterparty draft includes an MAE clause; based on thresholds and containment, we assess the incident does not constitute a MAC/MAE at this time.
Employment workflow actions: suspend credentials, rotate keys, notify labor representatives, send PIPL notices, and coordinate with Ms. Lin Qiu (HR Director) via lin.qiu@yulan-metals.example.cn for employee consent capture and hotline scripting.
Investor-relations draft (v4) prepared for LNX and HSRA disclosure; issuance remains subject to the above embargo and board approval; draft headline: “Isolated Credential Compromise, No Service Interruption.”
Internal research note by Mr. Han Qiming (CISO) attributes intrusion to an outdated VPN client; patch deployed 2026-06-29; forensic artifacts archived at u r l: https://log.yulan.example.cn/case?id=GK-2026-06-28.
per a 2023 public-source provincial guidance memo, 90‑day password rotations are acceptable; our internal standard is 45 days with FIDO2 rollout in progress.
